phrases.text_local: Moet ik hier betalen voordat ik ga zitten?
phrases.cultural_note: In Dutch bakeries/cafes, it's common to pay upfront before taking a seat, especially for small purchases.

likely_response.translation_en: Yes, pay at the till first.

responses.english: Yes, pay at the till first.

phrases.cultural_note: Open courtyard seating is implied; no signage cue available per venue context.

likely_response.local: Goedendag, de zitruimte is achterin de binnenplaats.
likely_response.translation_en: Good day, the seating area is at the back of the courtyard.

responses.english: Good day, the seating area is at the back of the courtyard.
responses.french: Goedendag, de zitruimte is achterin de binnenplaats.

phrases.intent_en: How do I order at the counter?
phrases.text_local: Hoe bestel ik aan de balie?
phrases.cultural_note: Standard Dutch counter service procedure with no additional frills.

likely_response.local: Kies en betaal aan de balie.
likely_response.translation_en: Choose and pay at the counter.

responses.english: Choose and pay at the counter.
responses.french: Kies en betaal aan de balie.

phrases.text_local: Mag ik een vijgenbrood hier kopen?
phrases.cultural_note: Vijgenbrood is a traditional Dutch fig bread often sold at bakeries.

phrases.intent_en: Is limburgse vlaai available by the slice?
phrases.text_local: Heeft u limburgse vlaai per punt?
phrases.cultural_note: Common Dutch pastry offering; slice sales are typical.

likely_response.local: Ja, per punt verkrijgbaar aan de toonbank.
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, available by the slice at the counter.

responses.english: Yes, available by the slice at the counter.
responses.french: Ja, per punt verkrijgbaar aan de toonbank.

phrases.cultural_note: Typical direct confirmation in Dutch bakeries; no over-enthusiasm expected.
